channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Simon Willison] Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
item.description: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML

item.title: [Hacker News] AI is breaking two vulnerability cultures
item.description: AI is breaking two vulnerability cultures

item.title: [Anthropic] Teaching Claude why
item.description: Teaching Claude why

item.title: [Hacker News] Podman rootless containers and the Copy Fail exploit
item.description: Podman rootless containers and the Copy Fail exploit

item.title: [OpenAI] Running Codex safely at OpenAI
item.description: Running Codex safely at OpenAI

item.title: [GitHub Trending] awslabs/aidlc-workflows — AI-Driven Life Cycle (AI-DLC) adaptive workflow steering rules for AI coding agents
item.description: AWS Labs released AIDLC-workflows, providing adaptive workflow steering rules for AI coding agents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] HKUDS/AI-Trader — "AI-Trader: 100% Fully-Automated Agent-Native Trading"
item.description: HKUDS released AI-Trader, a fully-automated trading system using agent-native architecture.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/lobehub — The ultimate space for work and life — to find, build, and collaborate with agent teammates that grow with you. We are taking agent harness to the next level — enabling multi-agent collaboration, effortless agent team design, and introducing agents as the unit of work interaction.
item.description: Lobehub introduces multi-agent collaboration platform for building and managing agent teams as primary work units.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] datawhalechina/hello-agents — 📚 《从零开始构建智能体》——从零开始的智能体原理与实践教程
item.description: A tutorial guide on building intelligent agents from scratch, covering foundational principles and hands-on implementation techniques.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] GPT-Realtime-2, -Translate, and -Whisper: new SOTA realtime voice APIs
item.description: [AINews] GPT-Realtime-2, -Translate, and -Whisper: new SOTA realtime voice APIs

item.title: [Hacker News] ClojureScript Gets Async/Await
item.description: ClojureScript Gets Async/Await

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Are Flat Minima an Illusion?
item.description: Paper challenges flat minima theory, proposing reparameterization-invariant "weakness" as true driver of neural network generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Nationwide EHR-Based Chronic Rhinosinusitis Prediction Using Demographic-Stratified Models
item.description: Nationwide EHR-based models with demographic stratification achieved 0.8461 AUC for predicting chronic rhinosinusitis diagnosis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SAT: Sequential Agent Tuning for Coordinator Free Plug and Play Multi-LLM Training with Monotonic Improvement Guarantees
item.description: SAT enables coordinator-free training of multiple smaller LLMs with monotonic improvement and plug-and-play agent swapping capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Learnable Loss Balancing and Transfer Learning
item.description: Framework introduces learnable loss balancing in PINNs to adaptively combine physics and data supervision for improved performance with limited data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Horizon-Constrained Rashomon Sets for Chaotic Forecasting
item.description: Research bridges predictive multiplicity and chaos theory, introducing horizon-constrained Rashomon sets that improve decision-making in chaotic forecasting systems by 18-34%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Sparse Prefix Caching for Hybrid and Recurrent LLM Serving
item.description: Sparse prefix caching stores recurrent LLM states at checkpoints rather than per-token positions, reducing serving latency for shared-prefix requests.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MidSteer: Optimal Affine Framework for Steering Generative Models
item.description: Researchers formalize concept steering theory and propose MidSteer, an affine framework for manipulating generative model representations with minimal disturbance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Data-Driven Variational Basis Learning Beyond Neural Networks: A Non-Neural Framework for Adaptive Basis Discovery
item.description: DVBL, a non-neural framework, learns data-adaptive basis functions through variational optimization while maintaining interpretability and mathematical transparency versus neural networks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Adaptive Computation Depth via Learned Token Routing in Transformers
item.description: Token-Selective Attention enables learned per-token routing in transformers, reducing computation by 14-23% while outperforming early exit methods with minimal quality loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Structural Instability of Feature Composition
item.description: Research reveals geometric instability when simultaneously activating multiple semantic features in Sparse Autoencoders, constraining compositional steering scalability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Channel-Level Semantic Perturbations: Unlearnable Examples for Diverse Training Paradigms
item.description: Researchers investigate unlearnable examples across training paradigms and propose Shallow Semantic Camouflage to maintain data privacy protection effectiveness with pretrained frozen weights.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MACS: Modality-Aware Capacity Scaling for Efficient Multimodal MoE Inference
item.description: MACS framework addresses straggler effects in multimodal MoE model inference through entropy-weighted load balancing and dynamic capacity allocation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Internalizing Outcome Supervision into Process Supervision: A New Paradigm for Reinforcement Learning for Reasoning
item.description: Novel reinforcement learning method converts outcome-only supervision into process-level learning signals by autonomously extracting fine-grained feedback from failed reasoning trajectories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Rethinking Data Curation in LLM Training: Online Reweighting Offers Better Generalization than Offline Methods
item.description: Online dynamic reweighting of training samples via adaptive learning rates improves LLM generalization better than offline data curation methods like selection and mixing.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Evolutionary fine tuning of quantized convolution-based deep learning models
item.description: Evolutionary optimization fine-tunes quantized models by adjusting weight quantization states, improving accuracy over standard nearest-neighbor quantization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Expert Routing for Communication-Efficient MoE via Finite Expert Banks
item.description: Information-theoretic framework uses algorithmic mutual information to analyze and optimize expert routing in sparse Mixture-of-Experts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Forecasting Green Skill Demand in the Automotive Industry: Evidence from Online Job Postings
item.description: Computational framework forecasts green skills in Mexico's automotive industry using Transformer models on job postings, identifying rapid growth in renewable energy competencies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Attribution-Guided Continual Learning for Large Language Models
item.description: Attribution-guided framework mitigates catastrophic forgetting in LLMs by using task-specific parameter importance to selectively update weights during sequential fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Graph Normalization: Fast Binarizing Dynamics for Differentiable MWIS
item.description: Graph Normalization is a differentiable dynamical system that approximates the NP-hard Maximum Weight Independent Set problem, always converging to binary solutions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Feature Starvation as Geometric Instability in Sparse Autoencoders
item.description: Adaptive elastic net sparse autoencoders address feature starvation in LLMs by combining L2 structural stability with adaptive L1 reweighting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Multi-Head Attention Approach for SLA Compliance Monitoring in Data Centers
item.description: Multi-head transformer models predict data center SLA violations 30 minutes in advance for proactive remediation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Balancing Stability and Plasticity in Sequentially Trained Early-Exiting Neural Networks
item.description: Sequential early-exit neural network training improves by constraining critical parameters and preserving output distributions of earlier exits.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] COPYCOP: Ownership Verification for Graph Neural Networks
item.description: CopyCop detects copied Graph Neural Networks despite different architectures, weights, and transformed embeddings.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SPADE: Faster Drug Discovery by Learning from Sparse Data
item.description: SPADE algorithm for drug discovery learns from sparse data to achieve 7-32% sample efficiency gains and 10x speed improvements over competitors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Neural Co-state Policies: Structuring Hidden States in Recurrent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Study links hidden states in recurrent reinforcement learning policies to optimal control's Pontryagin principle, proposing co-state loss for improved robustness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Conditional Diffusion Under Linear Constraints: Langevin Mixing and Information-Theoretic Guarantees
item.description: Study identifies measurement consistency gaps in conditional diffusion models and proposes projected-Langevin initialization for improved inverse problem sampling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Two-Stage Learned Decomposition for Scalable Routing on Multigraphs
item.description: A neural method using two-stage decomposition improves scalability and speed for vehicle routing on multigraphs while maintaining solution quality.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Differentiable Parameter Optimization for DAEs with State-Dependent Events
item.description: Two gradient computation methods enable parameter learning for differential-algebraic equations with state-dependent events and discontinuous mode transitions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Information Theoretic Adversarial Training of Large Language Models
item.description: WARDEN framework applies information-theoretic adversarial training to reduce LLM attack success rates while maintaining model utility.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Active Learning for Conditional Generative Compressed Sensing
item.description: Research on conditional generative compressed sensing proves prompts are design variables with distinct effects on Christoffel sampling and image recovery.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AdaGATE: Adaptive Gap-Aware Token-Efficient Evidence Assembly for Multi-Hop Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: AdaGATE improves multi-hop retrieval-augmented generation by repairing evidence gaps under token constraints, achieving better results with 2.6x fewer tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Counterargument for Critical Thinking as Judged by AI and Humans
item.description: Study shows students writing counterarguments exhibit critical thinking, and LLMs can reliably assess student work comparably to humans.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Generating Query-Focused Summarization Datasets from Query-Free Summarization Datasets
item.description: Researchers propose evidence-based method to automatically generate queries from query-free summarization datasets, achieving competitive performance on QFS tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SLAM: Structural Linguistic Activation Marking for Language Models
item.description: SLAM, a novel LLM watermarking scheme, embeds marks in linguistic structure rather than token frequencies, achieving 100% detection with minimal quality loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ReaComp: Compiling LLM Reasoning into Symbolic Solvers for Efficient Program Synthesis
item.description: Symbolic solvers compiled from LLM reasoning achieve 84.7% accuracy on hard program synthesis tasks without test-time LLM calls, reducing token usage by 78%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Chainwash: Multi-Step Rewriting Attacks on Diffusion Language Model Watermarks
item.description: Multi-step rewriting attacks significantly degrade watermark detection in diffusion language models, reducing detection from 87.9% to 4.86% after five chained rewrites.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Few Good Clauses: Comparing LLMs vs Domain-Trained Small Language Models on Structured Contract Extraction
item.description: Domain-trained small language model Olava Extract outperforms frontier LLMs on contract extraction while reducing inference costs by 78-97%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Cost of Context: Mitigating Textual Bias in Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: Study identifies 'recorruption' where accurate RAG context causes multimodal models to ignore visual information; proposes BAIR to restore visual grounding.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When2Speak: A Dataset for Temporal Participation and Turn-Taking in Multi-Party Conversations for Large Language Models
item.description: When2Speak dataset trains LLMs on turn-taking timing in multi-party conversations, achieving 60% average F1 improvement through supervised fine-tuning over zero-shot baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] One Turn Too Late: Response-Aware Defense Against Hidden Malicious Intent in Multi-Turn Dialogue
item.description: Researchers propose TurnGate, a detection system that identifies the earliest turn in multi-turn dialogues where responding would enable harmful attacks against LLMs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Negative Before Positive: Asymmetric Valence Processing in Large Language Models
item.description: LLMs process emotional valence asymmetrically—negative in early layers, positive in mid-to-late layers—both localized and steerable.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] XL-SafetyBench: A Country-Grounded Cross-Cultural Benchmark for LLM Safety and Cultural Sensitivity
item.description: XL-SafetyBench is a cross-cultural LLM safety benchmark with 5,500 test cases across 10 languages, finding jailbreak robustness doesn't guarantee cultural awareness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Decomposing the Basic Abilities of Large Language Models: Mitigating Cross-Task Interference in Multi-Task Instruct-Tuning
item.description: BADIT decomposes LLM parameters into orthogonal LoRA experts representing basic abilities to mitigate cross-task interference in multi-task instruct-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BioTool: A Comprehensive Tool-Calling Dataset for Enhancing Biomedical Capabilities of Large Language Models
item.description: BioTool dataset with 7,040 biomedical query-API pairs improves LLM tool-calling through fine-tuning, surpassing commercial models like GPT-5.1.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Estimating the Black-box LLM Uncertainty with Distribution-Aligned Adversarial Distillation
item.description: DisAAD uses lightweight proxy models to estimate black-box LLM uncertainty without requiring internal model access or multiple sampling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Evaluation Awareness in Language Models Has Limited Effect on Behaviour
item.description: Study of language models finds that evaluation awareness produces minimal behavioral effects, ranging from near-zero to 3.7 percentage points.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Steering Vector: Flow-based Activation Steering for Inference-Time Intervention
item.description: FLAS, a flow-based activation steering method, outperforms in-context prompting on AxBench by learning concept-conditioned velocity fields for language model control.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Logic-Regularized Verifier Elicits Reasoning from LLMs
item.description: Unsupervised verifier LOVER uses logical constraints to improve LLM reasoning without labeled data, matching supervised verifier performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Minimizing Modality Gap from the Input Side: Your Speech LLM Can Be a Prosody-Aware Text LLM
item.description: Researchers propose TextPro-SLM, which reduces speech-text modality gap by processing audio as prosody-aware text using ~1,000 hours of training data.
